description.p: CrossMacro helps you record and replay mouse and keyboard actions. It works on both Wayland and X11.

ul.li: Record and replay mouse movements, clicks, and keyboard events
Assign macros to keyboard shortcuts with toggle or hold-to-run modes
Schedule macros to run at specific times or intervals
Macro editor with undo/redo and action reordering
Playback with pause/resume, adjustable speed (0.1x to 10x), and loop mode
Save and load macros in .macro format
Customizable global hotkeys for control (defaults: F8 Record, F9 Play, F10 Pause)
CLI and headless workflows for automation and scripting
Multiple themes: Classic, Latte, Mocha, Dracula, Nord, Everforest, Gruvbox, Solarized, Crimson

description.p: CrossMacro works on both Wayland and X11. Absolute mouse positioning is supported on Hyprland, KDE Plasma, and GNOME.
On GNOME, a Shell Extension can be installed on first run. A logout/login may be required for the extension to take effect.

If permissions are missing, the app offers Quick Setup on launch. Quick Setup opens a standard system authorization prompt. If setup cannot complete, you can use the native daemon setup instructions in the project README.

screenshot.caption: Recording Tab - Record mouse and keyboard macros
Playback Tab - Play recorded macros with customizable settings
Text Expansion Tab - Create text shortcuts
Shortcuts Tab - Assign macros to keyboard shortcuts
Schedule Tab - Run macros at specific times
Editor Tab - Edit macro actions with reorder and undo/redo

description.p: CrossMacro 1.0.1 delivers major Linux workflow improvements for scripting and Wayland environments.

ul.li: Fixed a Linux mutex contention issue that could cause intermittent daemon synchronization failures.
Added Wayfire Wayland compositor support for absolute pointer position integration.
Added the current-position action for using live cursor coordinates in automation flows.
Expanded the script engine with structured control flow: if, for, while, continue, and break.

description.p: This release improves Linux packaging reliability and refreshes the application metadata for the 0.9.9 release.
This release adds full Flatpak support with Wayland compatibility.
